title: Cache rename — rule-test.index.json → .fabric/.cache/knowledge-test.index.json
scope: Path constants across packages/cli/, packages/server/, tests + .gitignore
description: Rename the legacy v1-vocabulary cache file `.fabric/rule-test.index.json` to v2-aligned location `.fabric/.cache/knowledge-test.index.json`. This is the last v1-vocabulary leak from rc.1 (`rule-test` references). Steps: (1) grep all literal `rule-test.index` occurrences across packages/, (2) update path constants to new location, (3) ensure `.fabric/.cache/` directory is created if missing during write, (4) add `.fabric/.cache/` to `.gitignore` (it's regenerable cache, should not be tracked), (5) update test fixtures asserting old path, (6) delete the old file from this repo's `.fabric/` (it's currently present per `ls .fabric/` output) — regen on next scan.

files.change: Locate path-constant for old `.fabric/rule-test.index.json`; update to `.fabric/.cache/knowledge-test.index.json`; ensure parent dir `.fabric/.cache/` is mkdir-recursive before write
Update any reads of `.fabric/rule-test.index.json` to new path; rename internal symbols if appropriate (e.g. `RULE_TEST_INDEX_PATH` → `KNOWLEDGE_TEST_INDEX_PATH`)
If watcher monitors the index file, update path; ensure `.fabric/.cache/**` watch pattern is correct
Add `.fabric/.cache/` ignore rule (regenerable cache should not be tracked); preserve existing entries
Delete the obsolete file from current working tree — next `fab scan` will regenerate at new location
Update any assertion of `.fabric/rule-test.index.json` to new path; update setup/teardown if old path was being created

implementation: Run `grep -rn 'rule-test\.index\|rule_test_index\|ruleTestIndex' packages/ tests/ scripts/ 2>/dev/null` to enumerate ALL call sites (literal + camelCase + snake_case)
Run `grep -rln 'rule-test' packages/cli/src/ packages/server/src/` to find all source files needing edits

Update each source file: path constant `'.fabric/rule-test.index.json'` → `'.fabric/.cache/knowledge-test.index.json'`; rename associated symbols (`RULE_TEST_INDEX_PATH` → `KNOWLEDGE_TEST_INDEX_PATH`, etc.)
Add `await mkdir('.fabric/.cache/', { recursive: true })` (or equivalent) before writing the index file
Update `.gitignore` — add `.fabric/.cache/` line; verify with `cat .gitignore | grep -q '.fabric/.cache/'`
Delete the existing `.fabric/rule-test.index.json` file: `git rm .fabric/rule-test.index.json` (committed deletion)
Update test fixtures: `grep -rln 'rule-test\.index' packages/cli/__tests__ packages/server/__tests__` and update each
Run `pnpm build && pnpm test` — confirm all green; tests should regenerate cache at new path during execution
Final grep gate: `grep -rE 'rule-test\.index' packages/` returns empty (all sites updated)

reference.pattern: v1-vocabulary cache rename — last leak from rc.1 cleanup
reference.examples: Follow rc.2-prep cleanup pattern (commits da80d5e..97103ab) — hard rename, no aliases/symlinks; .gitignore for cache directories

criteria: `grep -rE 'rule-test\.index' packages/` returns empty (all source sites updated)
`pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` exits 0
`git status .fabric/.cache/` shows directory ignored (not staged for commit)

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: any other v1-vocabulary leaks beyond rule-test.index (rc.2-prep already handled INITIAL_TAXONOMY, .fabric/rules, etc.); schema changes (TASK-002/003/004); docs (TASK-006). Strictly: rename one cache file + update path constants + .gitignore + test fixtures.

rationale.chosen_approach: Hard rename with explicit cache subdirectory (.fabric/.cache/) and gitignore — establishes a convention for future regenerable artifacts
alternatives_considered: Keep old name (preserves v1 vocabulary leak)
Rename in place without subdirectory (loses convention for future cache files)
Symlink old → new (legacy alias — rejected per clean-slate memory)
decision_factors: .fabric/.cache/ subdirectory provides clear opt-in path for future regenerable artifacts (forensic-cache, mcp-call-cache, etc.)
Hard rename matches clean-slate preference (zero-user migration tax)
rationale.tradeoffs: Existing checkouts will see deletion of old file and creation of new directory — accepted because zero users and the old file is regenerable on next scan

manual_checks: Run `pnpm scan` (or equivalent CLI command that triggers cache write) on a tmp project — confirm new file appears at `.fabric/.cache/knowledge-test.index.json`
Run `git status` after a fresh scan — confirm `.fabric/.cache/` is NOT shown as untracked (gitignored correctly)
Manually invoke server watcher — confirm watch path updated and old path is no longer monitored
success_metrics: Cache regenerates correctly at new location after fresh scan
unit: Path constant references match new location
mkdir-recursive on .fabric/.cache/ succeeds when parent missing
integration: End-to-end scan command writes index to new path
